threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'London Fields' instead of 'The Fields' and look shocked when someone tells you to get the bus to the Lido.
Caught a night bus and overheard a guy telling his mate he once mistook a kebab shop for a dating app. No one batted an eye.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw the same guy feeding the ducks at the canal every Sunday, I realized this place has its own rhythm. It's small moments like that.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You know those spontaneous street parties in Kreuzberg? One moment it's just a quiet road, then bam—a whole block is dancing under the stars, no planning, just vibes.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a true local by how they dodge the brunch crowds and hit the taco trucks at midnight instead.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a Muni bus today that turned into a pop-up dinner spot—two guys grilling next to the stop. Only in the Mission, right?

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is just a picturesque spot, but they miss the vibrant street art and the local bars that come alive after dark.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones still fumbling with the metro map, staring at it like it’s a puzzle. Locals just pull out their phones and get on with it.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'Stokey' instead of just 'The Nook' when suggesting a coffee spot. You'll learn soon enough!
Last Saturday on the 59, I overheard a guy confessing he once tried to pay with a bag of Percys. The driver didn’t even blink.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I saw a flock of pigeons casually fighting over a bratwurst on the sidewalk while a couple argued over their favorite Kebab spot. Classic Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You can find pop-up clubs in abandoned buildings here, but only during the full moon. It's like a secret rave calendar that only locals seem to know.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: A true local knows the difference between a burrito and a Mission-style burrito—it's all in the rice and the salsa, trust me.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a street performer on the N-Judah last week. He turned the whole car into an impromptu concert. Muni's chaos has a wild charm.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think every bar has the same late-night vibe, but locals know the quiet gems are where you’ll find the best conversations and tapas.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They always struggle to navigate the layout of Gràcia's plazas, sticking to the main roads instead of wandering the vibrant alleys.
